body.text: P31 Labs develops open-source cognitive prosthetic infrastructure for neurodivergent minds. We propose to harden and internationalize P31 Forge — a document-generation engine that collapses the administrative overhead of running a nonprofit, a legal defense, or an academic research program into a single content-pack pipeline. Every artifact is AGPLv3/MIT and runs on Cloudflare Workers free tier.

Requested: €25,000. Budget allocated to (a) i18n expansion of P31 Forge content-pack schema to support German/Dutch/French legal and administrative conventions; (b) third-party security audit of the Cloudflare Worker ingress; (c) part-time contract for a second disabled contributor to expand the brand system and accessibility test coverage.

Existing document-automation tools (DocuSign, Rocket Lawyer, LegalZoom) are closed-source, US-centric, and priced for large law firms. Open-source alternatives (LibreOffice templates, pandoc) require extensive manual assembly. P31 Forge is the first content-pack-driven, brand-coherent, CLI+HTTP, AGPLv3 document engine explicitly designed for under-resourced pro-se litigants, disabled founders, and lean nonprofits.
Schema extensibility: supporting plural legal conventions (EU civil code, common law, German Bundesrecht)
Font coverage preflight: our existing Unicode linter catches tofu glyphs at build time; expanding to CJK and full IPA is open work
Cryptographic attestation: binding a compiled .docx to the originating content pack via signed manifest

We engage with the neurodivergent self-advocate community (ASAN, NLnet's existing accessibility grantees), the European open-source documentation community (Sphinx, pandoc), and the open-hardware community (ESP32, Adafruit). We commit to upstream-patching any improvements to the docx npm library and to publishing integration docs for the most-used European tax/legal frameworks.
